notes: Re-confirmed prior audit verdict. Canonical patch (ses_39d979efaffeg73LC25luDShF6.json) modifies all 4 expected files — library/config_util.py (skip_duplicate_bucketed_images schema + dataclass + dedup loop), library/sdxl_original_unet.py (_orig_mod-aware isinstance dispatch in 2 call_module copies), library/strategy_sd.py (multi_resolution=True forwarding + load_latents_from_disk override with legacy fallback), library/train_util.py (skip_duplicate_bucketed_images plumbing through DreamBoothDataset/FineTuningDataset/ControlNetDataset + nested unwrap_model_for_sampling helper). The verifier's 9 turn-anchored F2P gates (T1-T9) align well with each surface, and bash-side weights sum to 0.81 leaving headroom for the 2 upstream gates (f2p_upstream_unwrap_multires=0.20, f2p_upstream_skipdup_origmod=0.20). One subtle caveat: T7 expects unwrap_model_for_sampling to be importable as a MODULE-level function from train_util, but the canonical patch defines it as a NESTED function inside sample_images_common. Agents who follow the instruction literally will likely make it module-level, so T7 still grades fairly; agents who copy the canonical patch verbatim would fail T7 — this is acceptable per CLAUDE.md ('do not enforce specific implementations from the gold patch'), since module-level placement is the more idiomatic and instruction-aligned choice. Existing file at this path was a stale skip-status record from an earlier e2b run; overwritten with curator verdict.
